mechanism_narrative: Sustained economic growth, as measured by GDPC1, indicates a healthy corporate earnings environment and robust consumer and business activity, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows consistent positive quarter-over-quarter growth, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat over a 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or coincides with economic contractions and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of such triggered instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with an aggressive monetary tightening stance (FEDFUNDS increasing rapidly) signals a challenging environment for corporate profitability and higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY AND FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25% in 1 month
failure_condition: If the specified inflation and FEDFUNDS conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns, particularly during periods of economic expansion.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% MoM during an economic expansion, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth suggests a weakening labor market, which can dampen consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 50k, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating robust economic output and supporting overall economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows a sustained positive trend (e.g., >0.1% MoM for 6 consecutive months), but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative during that period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a significant slowdown in the interest-sensitive housing market, which often precedes broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% MoM AND FEDFUNDS increases > 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% MoM while FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5% in 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of such occurrences over a 15-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a period of economic recovery provides accommodative financial conditions, supporting corporate investment and consumer borrowing, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% during a declared economic recovery, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 12-month period within that regime.
